pr.title: Update billing copy
pr.description: Refresh the Free plan copy on the Billing page so it's clearer about what upgrading unlocks.

visual_comparisons.summary: No changes detected between the baseline and the PR version.
The Settings interface has been redesigned from a full-page view to a modal overlay on top of the dashboard. Additionally, the 'Dark mode' option is missing from the settings list.
The description text below the main heading has been updated with new copy.

in_scope: Updating the Free plan billing copy on the Billing page (billing.html)
out_of_scope: Changing the Settings entry point on index.html from a link pointing to settings.html to a button that triggers a modal
Adding the Settings modal HTML markup and backdrop to index.html
Adding inline JavaScript functions (openSettings and closeSettings) to index.html to toggle the modal state
scope_analysis.reasoning: The PR contains major functional changes to the homepage (index.html), changing the Settings navigation behavior into an in-page modal interface. This is completely unrelated to the stated scope of updating the billing copy on the Billing page and represents undocumented scope creep.

code_review.summary: The PR contains significant out-of-scope changes. While the billing copy update in billing.html aligns with the stated scope, index.html has been modified to replace page navigation to settings.html with an in-page modal dialog, introducing undocumented UI behavior and JavaScript functions.
concerns: Modifications to index.html are entirely outside the declared scope of updating billing copy.
Changing the Settings entry from a link (navigating to settings.html) to a button opening a modal alters user workflow and could break existing routing or deep links.
Introduces new JavaScript functions (openSettings, closeSettings) and HTML structure for a modal in index.html without prior design approval or test updates.
Leaves the status of settings.html unclear, potentially creating dead code if it is no longer used.
